rationale: The cited paper is used as a central empirical benchmark rather than a passing background reference. The citing authors explicitly contrast their own findings with Zhu, Chen, and Dasgupta (2008), then run an extended replication of that study's design and scenario to explain discrepancies. They conclude that the earlier trade-in-dominance result depends on contextual cues and mind-set manipulations, which qualifies the scope of the cited paper's claims.
relation_description: The citing paper directly revisits and replicates Zhu, Chen, and Dasgupta (2008), then alters key scenario elements to test boundary conditions. It argues that the cited effect is conditional, refining when trade-ins increase focus and willingness to pay versus when consumers prioritize the new purchase.
